summary:
Vigabatrin is known to cause irreversible, progressive concentric visual field loss due to damage to retinal neurons. It has specific regulatory alerts for this side effect, distinguishing it among anticonvulsant medications. 

The summary above contains only clinically significant information directly related to the query about which anticonvulsant leads to visual field constriction. All non-essential details have been removed as per instructions.
